A TORNADO SNAPPED AND UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES...BLEW DOWN POWER POLES AND POWER LINES...DAMAGED SEVERAL HOMES...AND DESTROYED SEVERAL BARNS. ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS IN THE TORNADO WERE 95 TO 105 MPH...THUS THE TORNADO HAS BEEN RATED EF-1 ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND ABOUT 7.5 MILES AND WAS ABOUT 400 YARDS WIDE.

*** 4 INJ *** A TORNADO DESTROYED ONE MOBILE HOME...DAMAGED SEVERAL OTHER MOBILE HOMES...DAMAGED SEVERAL PERMANENT HOMES...DESTROYED BARNS...SNAPPED AND UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES...AND BLEW DOWN POWER POLES/POWER LINES. FOUR INJURIES OCCURRED. BASED ON THIS DAMAGE...MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS WERE 120-130 MPH...THUS THE TORNADO HAS BEEN RATED EF-2 ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE. PATH LENGTH IN OKLAHOMA WAS ABOUT 6.5 MILES. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS ABOUT 550 YARDS.

ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIND IN THE TORNADO WAS 70 TO 75 MPH BASED ON THIS DAMAGE...AND THUS IT WAS RATED EF0 ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE. IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR LESS THAN A MINUTE...WAS UP TO 150 YARDS WIDE...AND THE PATH LENGTH WAS ABOUT HALF A MILE.
